medium.description: Physical medium used by the link.

connector_type.description: Physical connector family (for example rj45, sfp, n/a).

max_length_m.description: Vendor or standard maximum supported length.

osi_layer.description: OSI layer classification for this class.

class.router.title: Router
class.router.description:
Base class for all routers with routing, NAT, firewall, and
optional VPN, WiFi, uplink policies, and advanced features.
Differentiation via capability packs (home_gateway, travel_vpn, enterprise).
invariants: object.enabled_capabilities must be subset of (required + optional)
instance.overrides must not disable required capabilities
if object.enabled_packs specified, object must support all pack capabilities

changes: Renamed router class namespace to class.router (simplified naming)
Expanded capability catalog from 10 to 49 capabilities
Added capability domains (routing, nat, firewall, vpn, wifi, wan, qos, services, advanced)
Split VPN capabilities into client vs server modes
Added WiFi 6 and repeater mode capabilities
Added multi-instance VPN and policy routing
Added DNS privacy capabilities (DoT, DoH, AdGuard)
Initial class definition in legacy network-scoped namespace

obj.glinet.slate_ax1800.title: GL.iNet Slate AX (GL-AXT1800)
obj.glinet.slate_ax1800.description:
GL.iNet Slate AX (GL-AXT1800) - Compact portable travel router with WiFi 6,
dual UI (GL.iNet + OpenWRT LuCI), advanced VPN client features, and WiFi
repeater mode. Perfect for travel, remote work, and secure hotel WiFi access.

indicators.name: power_led
indicators.description: RGB LED for status (white=normal, blue=WAN, red=error, green=VPN)

indicators.name: reset_button
indicators.description: Reset button (10s hold = factory reset, U-Boot recovery)

use_cases: Travel VPN gateway (hotel, airport, cafe WiFi)

setup: Connect hotel Ethernet to WAN port
Create WiFi AP for your devices
Enable WiFi repeater mode on wlan0 (5GHz)
Connect to hotel WiFi as client
Create WiFi AP on wlan1 (2.4GHz) for your devices
benefits: Single VPN connection protects all devices
No need to configure VPN on each device
Can connect devices without VPN support (IoT, consoles)

phone_tethering.description: Use phone 4G/5G when no WiFi available
setup: Create WiFi AP for your devices

usb_ethernet.description: Expand LAN ports with USB Ethernet adapter
setup: Connect USB Ethernet adapter to USB 3.0 port

limitations: No LTE modem (must use external USB LTE modem or phone tethering)
No REST API (must use LuCI or SSH)
CPU performance lower than MikroTik (MIPS vs ARM64)
advantages: Per-client VPN policy routing (easier than MikroTik PBR)
User-friendly GL.iNet UI for non-technical users
USB-C power input (can use phone charger)

obj.mikrotik.chateau_lte7_ax.title: MikroTik Chateau LTE7 AX
obj.mikrotik.chateau_lte7_ax.description:
MikroTik Chateau LTE7 AX - ARM64-based central home gateway with WiFi 6,
built-in LTE Cat 7 modem, RouterOS v7 container support, and comprehensive
routing/VPN features. Ideal for home/small office with failover requirements.

cellular.name: lte1
cellular.description: LTE Cat 7 modem for WAN failover

use_cases: Central home gateway with LTE failover
Home lab gateway with container support
Self-hosted services gateway (AdGuard, Tailscale in containers)
limitations: No PoE output on LAN ports
WiFi repeater mode not officially supported (RouterOS limitation)

obj.mikrotik.chr.title: MikroTik CHR (Cloud Hosted Router)
obj.mikrotik.chr.description:
Virtual RouterOS appliance for cloud or virtualized deployments.
Uses the same router capability contract as physical MikroTik routers,
without hardware-specific features such as WiFi or LTE modem.

changes: Removed legacy capability IDs (old flat NAT/WireGuard/VLAN aliases)
Added required cap.net.service.dns.resolver for class.router compliance
